heading: Research program on quiet aircraft technology for propeller and rotor driven aircraft
label: 47509 Research program on quiet aircraft technology for propeller and rotor driven aircraft
notes: Amendments1996—Subsec. (d). substituted “” for “the date of the enactment of this section”.Pub. L. 104–287August 23, 1994
amendments: Amendments1996—Subsec. (d). substituted “” for “the date of the enactment of this section”.Pub. L. 104–287August 23, 1994

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: The Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration and the Administrator of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration shall conduct a study to identify technologies for noise reduction of propeller driven aircraft and rotorcraft.

children.heading: .—Goal
children.label: b .—Goal
children.text: The goal of the study conducted under subsection (a) is to determine the status of research and development now underway in the area of quiet technology for propeller driven aircraft and rotorcraft, including technology that is cost beneficial, and to determine whether a research program to supplement existing research activities is necessary.

children.heading: .—Participation
children.label: c .—Participation
children.text: In conducting the study required under subsection (a), the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration and the Administrator of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration shall encourage the participation of the Department of Defense, the Department of the Interior, the airtour industry, the aviation industry, academia and other appropriate groups.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: d .—Report
children.text: Not less than 280 days after , the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration and the Administrator of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration shall transmit to Congress a report on the results of the study required under subsection (a).August 23, 1994

children.heading: .—Research and Development Program
children.label: e .—Research and Development Program
children.text: If the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration and the Administrator of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration determine that additional research and development is necessary and would substantially contribute to the development of quiet aircraft technology, then the agencies shall conduct an appropriate research program in consultation with the entities listed in subsection (c) to develop safe, effective, and economical noise reduction technology (including technology that can be applied to existing propeller driven aircraft and rotorcraft) that would result in aircraft that operate at substantially reduced levels of noise to reduce the impact of such aircraft and rotorcraft on the resources of national parks and other areas.
